text:
interlocutory applications before this Court. The decision not 
to live with the Appellant was solely taken by Aadith, out of his 
own free will and based on the legal advice he received.
(c) 
Respondent No. 4, as Aadith’s primary caregiver, has safeguarded 
his son’s best interests and welfare in India. He has been provided 
with a stable, nurturing, and supportive environment where he 
has been encouraged to express his opinions and exercise his 
autonomy. In comparison, under the Appellant’s care, Aadith’s 
social, physical, and psychological growth were being impeded 
due to the Appellant’s constant dismissal of Aadith’s capacities. 
With Respondent No. 4’s guidance, Aadith has been undergoing 
requisite sessions for skill training for persons with disabilities 
in Chennai, is living under the loving embrace of his paternal 
family, and is able to showcase greater autonomy, thus attesting 
to his best interests and welfare being served.
